data.content: I see a tombstone with 7 frames in `libmonosgen-2.0.so` (BuildId: `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`). To symbolicate, I need the debug symbols for that binary. Let me try to fetch them from Microsoft's symbol server and use `llvm-symbolizer`.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try Microsoft symbol server - ELF buildid format
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
LIB="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

# Try downloading the debug symbols
curl -sSL -o /tmp/monosgen_debug.so \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${LIB}/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${LIB}" \
  -w "\nHTTP status: %{http_code}\nSize: %{size_download}\n" 2>&1

echo "---"

# Also try alternate URL format
curl -sSL -o /tmp/monosgen_debug2.so \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${LIB}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${LIB}" \
  -w "\nHTTP status: %{http_code}\nSize: %{size_download}\n" 2>&1
arguments.description: Download debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in the backtrace. The tombstone shows frames from `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with a BuildId of `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`. 

To symbolicate these frames, I would need:
1. The debug symbols for that specific build of `libmonosgen-2.0.so` (matching the BuildId)
2. A tool like `llvm-symbolizer`, `addr2line`, or similar to resolve the addresses to function names

Let me think about what's available. The user has the tombstone file but I don't see any symbol files or debug binaries in the directory. 

Without the matching debug symbols, I can't actually perform the symbolication. However, for a Mono runtime library like this, the symbols are typically available through Microsoft's symbol server, NuGet packages containing the runtime, or the .NET SDK installation. so.dbg` format. Let me try downloading from the symbol server, though this might be synthetic test data that won't actually exist on the server. I'll need `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` to resolve the addresses once I have the symbols.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5

# Try Microsoft symbol server - ELF buildid format
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
LIB="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

# Try downloading the debug symbols
curl -sSL -o /tmp/monosgen_debug.so \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${LIB}/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${LIB}" \
  -w "\nHTTP status: %{http_code}\nSize: %{size_download}\n" 2>&1

echo "---"

# Also try alternate URL format
curl -sSL -o /tmp/monosgen_debug2.so \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${LIB}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${LIB}" \
  -w "\nHTTP status: %{http_code}\nSize: %{size_download}\n" 2>&1
arguments.description: Download debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
